canonical_definition: Linear Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA) is a method for measuring the similarity between neural network representations, which the paper considered as an alternative analysis tool.
papers: DOCS: Quantifying Weight Similarity for Deeper Insights into Large Language Models
Emergence of a High-Dimensional Abstraction Phase in Language Transformers
